title: funastrum gracile
intro: Funastrum gracile, also known as Cane Cholla or Devil's Head, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Funastrum gracile include relief from respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. In herbal preparations, Funastrum gracile is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
